chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-4770 of 2024 Date Order with signature of Judge FRESH CASE. 1. For orders on Misc. No.21421/2024. 2. For orders on Office Objection No.01, 02 & 03. 3. For orders on Misc. No.21422/2024. 4. For orders on Misc. No.21423/2024. 5. For hearing of main case. 02.10.2024. Petitioner is present in person. ---- 1. Granted. 2-5. The Petitioner professes to be a Member of the Managing Committee of the Pakistan Hindu Council, arrayed as the Respondent No.8, with it being alleged that its affairs are being willfully mismanaged by the remaining members of its Committee, impleaded as the Respondents Nos.11 to 24, who are said to be acting contrary to the interests of the Council at the behest and instruction of the Respondent No.9, who is said to be its patron-in-chief and a prominent political figure. In that backdrop, it has been prayed that directions be issued to the Respondents Nos. 3 to 5, being the various investigative/anti-graft agencies to conduct an enquiry into the affairs of the Council. However, whilst the Council is apparently a body registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, the Petitioner has not approached the Registrar of the Societies, who exercises oversight as to its affairs, nor has that functionary even been impleaded as a respondent. On query posed to Counsel for the Petitioner as to whether any complaint or representation has been made to the Registrar, no cogent response was forthcoming, and reference was merely 2 made to a Complaint dated 20.09.2024 addressed to the Respondents Nos.3 to 5, which too appears to be cast in vague terms, bereft of material particulars.

However, whilst the Council is apparently a body registered under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, the Petitioner has not approached the Registrar of the Societies, who exercises oversight as to its affairs, nor has that functionary even been impleaded as a respondent. On query posed to Counsel for the Petitioner as to whether any complaint or representation has been made to the Registrar, no cogent response was forthcoming, and reference was merely 2 made to a Complaint dated 20.09.2024 addressed to the Respondents Nos.3 to 5, which too appears to be cast in vague terms, bereft of material particulars. Under such circumstances, the Petition does not present a fit case for issuance of a writ and accordingly stands dismissed in limine along with the pending Miscellaneous Applications. JUDGE JUDGE MUBASHIR
